channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Humanoid robot 'Sally' to teach AI to 11th and 12th grade students in New York school in a first-ever experiment in US
item.description: Sally, a humanoid robot teacher, is set to debut at a New York high school, providing personalized educational experiences in specialized classes. Designed to be relatable with her realistic appearance and western New York accent, Sally will support both students and teachers with curriculum-aligned learning and translations. Additionally, the school has taken steps to reassure parents and the local community about the integration of artificial intelligence in the classroom.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: An exoplanet found 48 light-years away has given scientists hope for life, here's why
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: A dog ate marijuana on Scotland's highest mountain, got too high to stand and had to be carried down by mountain rescuers
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'They should pay us': Trump threatens Canada PM with tariffs over 'poisoned air' affecting US cities
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: In 1948, Idaho dropped 76 beavers by parachute into remote wilderness, and they soon began building dams
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists used children’s ‘silly sprinklers’ to solve Feynman's physics problem
item.description: Mathematicians have solved a physics puzzle using backyard sprinklers. Experiments with silly sprinklers clarified how reverse sprinklers rotate. The key lies in how water momentum collides inside the central chamber. This principle applies across various sprinkler arm shapes and designs. The findings offer insights for fluid dynamics in engineering applications.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: How 58 beavers revived a valley buried by Mount St. Helens eruption
item.description: More than 40 years after the Mount St. Helens eruption buried a Washington river valley under volcanic sediment, 58 relocated beavers have helped restore the damaged landscape. Their dams and wetlands brought back native plants, fish and wildlife. However, conservationists fear a planned government project to raise a sediment retention structure could undo years of natural restoration.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists played human voices near Alaska salmon streams and found that bears and eagles fled
item.description: New findings from Canadian researchers reveal that human sounds near Alaska's salmon streams significantly disturb wildlife, leading to grizzly bears avoiding feeding areas. This interference disrupts the natural nutrient transfer to surrounding forests, emphasizing the detrimental effects of human activity on essential ecosystem dynamics. Scientists propose enhanced land management practices to safeguard these critical ecological processes.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Mount Olympus, the mythological home of Zeus and the ancient Greek gods, is being considered for UNESCO World Heritage status as Greece seeks protection for its tallest mountain
item.description: Mount Olympus, a site of myth and nature, seeks UNESCO World Heritage recognition. This ancient Greek mountain is home to gods and diverse ecosystems. Locals hope for global acknowledgment to ensure its continued protection. The nomination process involves careful review and potential further requirements. Recognition would bring responsibilities for preserving its unique cultural and natural values.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Archaeologists unearthed a rare 2,600-year-old Assyrian stele, a decade after ISIS damaged the city
item.description: A stunning 2,600-year-old Assyrian monument has surfaced in Nineveh, Iraq, celebrating the architectural feats of King Ashurbanipal. Unearthed amidst restoration efforts at the Shamash Gate, this stone artifact reveals essential details about Assyrian urban development. This significant find stands as a testament to the resilience of cultural identities, aiding in reconciling historical disruptions and preservation.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Bumblebees survive pesticide exposure, but reproductive genes begin changing
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: MacKenzie Scott once fixed a broken tooth with glue. Now she's giving $20 million to struggling teenagers
item.description: Philanthropist MacKenzie Scott gave a record $20 million to Active Minds. This donation addresses the critical youth mental health crisis in America. Scott's past financial struggles influenced her generous giving approach. The funding will expand mental health programs across schools and universities. Active Minds aims to reduce stigma and improve support accessibility for young people.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Warming seas are bringing octopus, red mullet and bream into UK waters
item.description: As the seas warm, British waters are now seeing an influx of Mediterranean species. This environmental transformation is compelling local fishers to modify their traditional techniques. Notably, octopus populations are surging, impacting native shellfish and depleting other seafood supplies. In response, chefs are creatively revamping menus to highlight these new arrivals like octopus and bream, balancing the challenges with exciting culinary possibilities.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: The real-life Gilmore Girls house from the pilot episode is for sale for $2.7 million in Ontario, where Unionville first stood in for Stars Hollow
item.description: A historic Unionville home, which served as Lorelai and Rory Gilmore’s house in the pilot episode, is now for sale. This property, listed at $3.88 million CAD, offers a tangible connection to the beloved show. The surrounding neighborhood also featured as key Stars Hollow locations, enhancing its appeal. The house, built around 1875, blends Victorian charm with modern amenities for comfortable living.
item.dc:creator: TOI Lifestyle Desk

item.title: Florida is planting 15,000 milkweeds along highways to help monarch butterflies
item.description: Scientists and transport officials in Florida have planted 15,000 milkweed plants along roadsides to help monarch butterflies survive and reproduce. Milkweed is the only plant on which monarchs lay their eggs, making it essential for their life cycle. The project also supports bees and other pollinators while testing whether roadside habitats can become long-term wildlife corridors across the state.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Arizona mass shooting: 10 injured after gunman opens fire in Tucson
item.description: A mass shooting in crowded downtown Tucson wounded at least ten people early Sunday. Police officers on patrol heard gunfire and responded to the crowded entertainment district. They encountered and shot the suspected gunman, who sustained life-threatening injuries. Nine other adults were shot and taken to hospitals in critical condition.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Democritus: 'If your desires are not great, a little will seem much to you'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Confucius: 'If I am walking with two other men, each of them will serve...'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Larry Ellison once struggled to pay his electricity bills. Now, he has written a $300 million cheque to...
item.description: Once grappling with utility bills for his workshop, tech magnate Larry Ellison transformed his fortunes by acquiring 98 percent of a Hawaiian island for a staggering $300 million. Now residing full-time on Lanai, he envisions a luxury destination coupled with a sustainable community. Despite fluctuations in his wealth driven by market trends, Oracle's cloud operations showcase remarkable strength amid ongoing challenges.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: IShowSpeed performs 'Champions' at FIFA World Cup 2026 final closing ceremony
item.description: IShowSpeed performed his song "Champions" at the FIFA World Cup 2026 final closing ceremony. He became the first content creator to perform live at this major global sporting event. His viral World Cup anthem gained significant popularity among football fans worldwide. FIFA secured rights to his song, which became a recognized tournament track. This performance highlighted the growing influence of online creators on international stages.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy
